data.epi_content:
"So, if you're sorry, please move aside, Vivian.

I don't know if you once felt like we were close, but we're certainly not now."

But Vivian just bit her lip slightly and continued staring at me.

From what I could see, she had no intention of moving.

So when I stood up and tried to go to my room, Vivian grabbed the sleeve of my right arm.

Perhaps it was because she had just grabbed my bare hand—well, it had been quite a while since she'd grabbed my bare hand and gotten slapped for it.

Thanks to her grip, my sleeve stuck to my arm, pressing against the wound and causing blood to seep out.

I could feel it dripping down my fingertips, drop by drop.

"Vivian. Have you lost your mind? Let go—"

"What is this?"

Vivian's grip on my sleeve tightened.

The more she pulled, the more the makeshift white cloth covering my wound came undone.

It seemed like the bleeding was just starting to stop, with scabs beginning to form here and there.

Was it just my imagination?

Anyway, the blood was now visibly seeping through my clothes, drop by drop.

Let's blame this all on Vivian grabbing my arm.

"Vivian, it hurts. Could you please let go?"

"Erica, your arm is bleeding! What on earth is this? No, you need treatment first!"

Vivian made a fuss and tried to either roll up my sleeve or remove my top altogether.

Whatever she was trying to do, I knew she was going to touch me, so I struck her arm away with all my strength.

But did she deliberately let me slap her earlier too?

Or did she just not expect me to hit her and took the slap?

It seems like the former.

She firmly grabbed my arm as I tried to push her away and lifted it up.

As a result, blood began to trickle down inside my arm.

Of course, my snow-white uniform was getting stained red along that path.

"There's no need to worry.

It hurts, so let go quickly. I'll go to my room and wrap it with a bandage, and it'll heal soon."

I tried to somehow shake Vivian off, but she held onto me and wouldn't let go.

It's a terrible feeling. To think I've become so weak that I can't free myself from a peer's grip.

"This, this doesn't look like something that will heal soon!!"

"You haven't seen it with your own eyes.

Vivian, though you haven't experienced it yet, you're quite a dull person who can't notice things unless you see them directly.

And you always approach people who don't want you to come near, which only makes them feel inferior..."

I was trying to somehow calm down Vivian, who looked like she was about to tear my sleeve and check the wound, so I could return to my room, but she wouldn't listen at all.

Like a puppeteer controlling a powerless puppet, Vivian grabbed my sleeve and exposed my arm.

"Miss, anyone can see this is..."

"I said it hurts, let go."

My wrist felt like it was about to be crushed in Vivian's grip.

But regardless, Vivian was just staring seriously at my arm, where the white cloth had fallen off and blood was flowing steadily.

The red drops rising up looked almost like flower buds pushing through skin, which was strangely captivating.

"Did you do this to yourself...?"
data.epi_content2:
When Vivian said that, my expression crumbled instantly.

I had been trying to maintain a composed face, wearing a mask.

"Ah."

Why did she have to say that out loud?

It's natural for others to see me as a mentally ill person who cuts her arms.

But hearing it directly makes me dizzy and want to shut her mouth immediately.

If I said, "I was punishing the other me inside!" she'd think I have another person inside me and that I'm a mentally ill person who cuts my own arm, so yes, I guess I am mentally ill.

I suppose it's understandable that she's acting this way, since what appeared to be a normal person just moments ago, albeit emotional, has now revealed herself to be mentally ill.

But while it might have been just moments ago for Vivian, I've already spent countless days and times beyond that.

Times that were sometimes terrible and sometimes filled with happiness as I enjoyed chocolate with coffee.

So, without realizing it, I became upset.

No one knows how hard it's been for me, how much I've suffered.

And I don't want to whine about it and ask for understanding.

Well, if I were to say it, they might search their memories and realize it's true, then be shocked and sympathize with my miserable life, regretting how they treated me harshly... it's an appealing thought, but a bit childish.

It's just a momentary emotional game, and the more stimulating experiences you have, the more you seek greater stimulation.

At least for me, time seems infinite, so what meaning would such actions have?

If I die miserably, they should just spit on my grave, thinking I'm a useless, disgusting, contemptible human being.

Because I'm jealous and envious but lack the ability to achieve what I want.

So, filled with all kinds of displeasure, I say:

"Let go."

"If you were in my position, could you let go?!"

"I could and more. I have nothing left, while you have so much that you can't even let go of others."

"I don't understand what you're saying, but you need treatment first!!"

"I don't care if your arms and legs are blown off, just go see a psychiatrist!!"

For a moment, I heard an auditory hallucination overlapping.

It made me feel nauseous, and I retched.

"Ughek..."

Fortunately, I hadn't eaten anything, so only stomach acid came pouring out.

It felt like my esophagus was burning.

In front of Vivian, who showed no intention of letting go, I couldn't say anything and only felt the tingling pain in my arm.

In the end, I chose once again to deny and run away from the jealousy, envy, and desire to not appear weak in front of her.

In other words, I began to kneel and beg in front of Vivian.

"Pl...please let go, no, please let me go. It's painful."

All I could do was cry.

In this situation, I wanted to escape, and somehow my pitiful appearance was being painted in my mind even though I couldn't see it.

Vivian's grip loosened.

Looking at my tightly gripped wrist, it was clear it would be bruised for days.

After staring at my tingling arm for a while, I pulled down the sleeve that Vivian had undone to cover the wound and stood up.

As if nothing had happened.

Yes, nothing happened.
data.epi_content3:
I never knelt before Vivian and begged her to let go.

There's no way I would do that, haha.

Then I picked up the cigar I had dropped on the floor, dusted it off, and put it in my mouth.

It had been on the floor for 3 seconds, but it's not like I was going to eat it, so it didn't matter.

When I inhaled the smoke deeply, my head spun and my lungs felt like they were rotting.

Originally, cigars weren't meant to be inhaled deeply.

Vivian was still in shock and didn't say anything to me.

"Next time, I'll remain the lady you know.

I'm sorry for shattering your illusion."

Yes, next time.

"What does that even mean...?"

I gently pushed aside Vivian, who was staring at me with her mouth wide open in bewilderment, and turned around to walk away.

Blood was dripping from my arm, probably because Vivian had gripped it so tightly.

If I went to my room and blew my head off, time would rewind.

How many times would I have to do it?

Let's go back to before I slapped Vivian's cheek.

And then try to live somehow until I die of old age.

Staying holed up in my room at the Academy, trying not to catch Lydia's eye.

Avoiding places where Vivian or Evan might go.

And if I do run into them, telling them to ignore me and somehow escaping from there.

"I suppose so."

But the option of returning to my room alone and blowing my head off to start anew suddenly disappeared.

While I was puffing on the cigar, inhaling the smoke and coughing as my insides burned, Vivian approached me and blocked my path.

"Um, could you please move aside?"

"No. I'm going to call Evan, so wait in the room."

Saying that, she also took the cigar from my hand and threw it somewhere, then picked me up.

Yes, she just grabbed me with her hands, positioned me so that the wounded area wouldn't be touched, and lifted me up!

I don't know if she enhanced her body with a spell, but being lifted by a peer without being able to resist wasn't exactly a pleasant experience.

The situation was so absurd that I just kept laughing.

Like a deranged person, or someone who found the situation too funny to contain their laughter.

"Ha, haha. Ha. Hee, hiccup."

Then suddenly I became sad and cried.

Fortunately, my crying was masked by my laughter and sounded like I was just gasping for breath after laughing.

Having already knelt and wrung my hands, crying didn't seem like a big deal, but these things aren't decided by rational thinking.

Especially for a fool who has feelings but no ability.

Ah. I feel powerless.

The world is cursed.

So much that I want to burn it all down.

Though I couldn't possibly do that.

Since it was just the empty thought of an incompetent human with emotions but no ability to act, I pushed it to the back of my mind.

Besides, my solar plexus was pressed against Vivian's shoulder, making it hard to breathe.

Maybe I was also lightheaded from losing so much blood.

Anyway, I just closed my eyes and let my body go limp.

Vivian hoisted me onto her shoulder and began carrying me somewhere.
